text: Here's what science actually says about platelets clotting. When you get a cut, tiny cells called platelets spring into action. They rush to the site of injury, sticking together to form a plug. Recent studies show that this process isn't just random; it’s a highly coordinated dance. Understanding this helps scientists develop better treatments for bleeding disorders. Curious about more science secrets? Hit that follow button!
